title: The Flame That Speaks Wrong
description: Ironhaven's temple flame has burned straight up for six centuries. Three weeks ago it started to lean. East. Precisely east. It has a direction now. Sella can hear it saying something, in pieces.

offer.speaker: Temple Keeper Sella
offer.text: The flame has burned straight up for six centuries. Three weeks ago it started to lean. East. Precisely east. It has not moved since. It has a direction now.

offer.speaker: Temple Keeper Sella
offer.text: I am not asking whether that is normal. I am asking what is on the other side of the direction it is pointing.

accept.speaker: Temple Keeper Sella
accept.text: Go and stand in front of it. Do not stand too close. The flame is *saying something*. In pieces. I have not been able to hear the whole sentence.

accept.speaker: Temple Keeper Sella
accept.text: If someone is at the flame when you arrive, listen to what THEY say too. The two might be the same sentence at different speeds.

decline.speaker: Temple Keeper Sella
decline.text: Then I will keep listening. The flame is patient. So am I. So, I suspect, is whatever is on the other end of it.

in_progress.speaker: Temple Keeper Sella
in_progress.text: There is a woman at the flame now. She is tending it. I did not appoint her. Nobody appointed her. She is tending anyway.

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Temple Keeper Sella
ready_to_turn_in.text: The flame is straight again. It is quiet. I have thoughts. Come sit with them.

complete.speaker: Temple Keeper Sella
complete.text: The flame is straight. It does not speak now. I miss it more than I expected to. What she said before you sent her away — did she say it in words, at the end? She rarely used words at the beginning.

complete.speaker: Temple Keeper Sella
complete.text: The flame was pointing at the capital. I have looked at the map three times to be certain. The capital. I would like to be uncertain about this. I am not.

complete.speaker: Temple Keeper Sella
complete.text: Take this ember. It is not lit, but it is warm. If it starts to lean, in your pocket, in your pack — put it down and look east.

objectives.description: Hear Temple Keeper Sella out at the Ironhaven temple.
Approach the tilted flame at the temple altar. Listen.
Confront the woman tending the flame — the one no one appointed.
Return to Sella at the temple entrance.

bard.description: Bard examine text at the flame: the fragments the flame is speaking are in a meter. Trochaic. The Bard recognizes the meter — it's the same meter as the chancellor's edicts, the ones the town crier reads. Someone is teaching a flame to recite. 'That's not a flame's cadence. That's a court's cadence.' No mechanical effect.

cross_world_connection: The leaning_ember item, if kept, leans in the party's pack whenever they enter a chamber where Mordaine's magic is present. In Castle Harmonia's throne room, it points straight at the plain chair beside the overturned throne (world1_throne_room_approach beat). Diegetic compass to the actual power center.
_wiring_notes: Step 2 custom flag emitted by an examine tile in front of the flame (cowir-overworld places; hook = 'The flame is saying something. In pieces.'). Step 3 shared flag w1_curator_defeated wired to Curator Masterite encounter (Lane B). Item leaning_ember = new item (flavor: 'An ember that leans. In your pack. Warm to the touch. Not lit.'). Design doc: Lane C row 4.
